aliases_seen: airborne ultrasound, airborne ultrasonics, non-contact ultrasound
one_line_definition: Ultrasonic measurement using air as the coupling medium between transducer and object; avoids liquid couplant
Single-transducer configuration where the same element transmits and receives the reflected echo
Two-transducer configuration with separate transmitter and receiver; used for transmission or oblique scattering measurements
Assumes local plane-wave reflection at each surface point; valid when radius of curvature >> λ; gives coherent intensity I ∝ exp(−k²Rq²(cosθᵢ+cosθᵣ)²/2)
Component of scattered field that retains phase coherence; its amplitude decays as Rayleigh factor exp(−(2kσcosθ)²) with roughness
Incoherent scattered energy distributed across angles; sensitive to both rms roughness Rq and surface correlation length λ₀
Square root of mean-squared surface height deviation from mean plane; primary vertical roughness parameter
Lateral length over which surface height fluctuations become uncorrelated; controls angular spread of diffuse scattering
P = 2kσcosθ₀; surface is acoustically smooth (specular) when P << 1, rough (diffuse) when P >> 1
Product of rms roughness and frequency used as master parameter for coherent ACU; Kirchhoff model valid for Rq·f < 100 μm·MHz
First-order expansion of boundary condition at rough surface about mean plane; valid when kσ << 1 and kσ·slope << 1
Complete second-order statistical description of a random rough surface using both vertical (Rq) and lateral (λ₀) parameters
Surface whose height probability distribution deviates from Gaussian; characterised by skewness Rsk and kurtosis Rku

aliases_seen: third central moment, height distribution asymmetry
one_line_definition: Third standardised moment of surface height distribution; positive for peaks-dominated surfaces, negative for valleys-dominated
Fourth standardised moment of surface height distribution; Rku = 3 for Gaussian; >3 for spiky surfaces
Flexible four-parameter probability distribution used to model non-Gaussian surface heights in ultrasonic roughness models
Empirical scaling Rq' = CF × Rq to correct Kirchhoff model for non-Gaussian surfaces; CF = 1.864/(1.864+0.059|Rsk|) (Saniman 2016)
AS = R(θ̄ᵢ)·exp(−8π²f²Rq²/V₁²); models amplitude loss due to roughness scattering as exponential function of f²Rq²
Geometric factor accounting for rough-surface facets that are hidden from illumination or observation at non-normal incidence; important above ~20° incidence angle
Statistical model of shadowing probability for randomly rough surfaces assuming single-scattering; Wagner (1967) JASA 41(1)
Extension of shadowing model to include multiple-scattering effects between surface facets; McCoy (1989) JASA 86(4)
Normalised ultrasonic echo amplitude as a function of beam angle; monotone increase with Rq at θ ≥ 20°
Two-dimensional Fourier transform of angular echo distribution used to characterise surface step height from peak spatial frequency
Discrete Fourier transform for irregularly sampled data; used when beam-steering angle spacing is non-uniform
Electronic control of transducer array phase delays to steer ultrasonic beam through angular range without mechanical movement
Microfabricated ultrasonic transducer using electrostatic (capacitive) actuation; low acoustic impedance makes it suitable for air coupling
Piezoelectric film transducer with broad bandwidth (10 kHz–1 MHz) suitable for air-coupled measurements
Transducer with piezoceramic rods in polymer matrix; low acoustic impedance improves matching to air; used for air-coupled NDE
Z_air ≈ 428 Pa·s/m vs Z_steel ≈ 46×10⁶ Pa·s/m; transmission coefficient ~2×10⁻⁵ in pressure; requires efficient transducers for ACU
Time for an ultrasonic pulse to travel from transmitter to receiver; primary measurement quantity for range, velocity, and thickness

aliases_seen: sound velocity in air, acoustic velocity
one_line_definition: c = √(γP/ρ) ≈ 331 + 0.606t m/s at dry air; depends on T, P, humidity, CO2; ~343 m/s at 20°C, 1 atm, 50% RH
16-coefficient polynomial giving c as function of t (°C), p (kPa), x_w, x_c; uncertainty < 300 ppm; JASA 93(5) 1993
Temperature inferred from sound speed assuming dry air; overestimates true temperature in humid air due to lower molecular weight of H₂O
Irreversible viscous and thermal conduction losses; α_cr = 1.83×10⁻¹¹(T/T₀)^(1/2) f²/(P/P₀) Np/m; dominant above ~500 kHz

aliases_seen: O₂ relaxation, N₂ relaxation, relaxation absorption
one_line_definition: Energy exchange between translational and vibrational modes of O₂ and N₂; produces humidity-dependent absorption peak at audio/low-ultrasonic frequencies
Characteristic frequency of O₂ vibrational relaxation; strongly humidity-dependent; controls position of absorption maximum in 1–100 kHz range
Characteristic frequency of N₂ vibrational relaxation; less humidity-sensitive than O₂; contributes secondary absorption peak at higher f/P
International standard formula for total atmospheric sound absorption (classical + vibrational relaxation); valid ~50 Hz–10 kHz; extended to ~1 MHz in Vladisauskas 2004

aliases_seen: air absorption, propagation loss in air
one_line_definition: Total sound attenuation in air from classical + molecular relaxation mechanisms; depends on f, T, P, RH
Absorption vs RH is non-monotone at audio frequencies: maximum at humidity where f = f_r; above ~500 kHz humidity effect negligible
Sound speed first decreases then increases with RH at audio frequencies (minimum at ~14% RH, 20°C); above 30% RH increases linearly; due to O₂ relaxation + lighter H₂O molecule
Frequency dependence of c arising from vibrational relaxation; related to absorption via Kramers-Kronig; causes c to decrease at fixed audio frequency as humidity increases
Mathematical relations linking frequency-dependent phase velocity to absorption spectrum; allow c(f) to be derived from α(f) and vice versa
c_h/c_0 = 1 + h(9.66×10⁻⁴ + ...) polynomial for equilibrium sound speed ratio vs humidity h and temperature t; valid 0–30°C, 0–100% RH

concept_term: specific heat ratio (γ) in moist air
aliases_seen: ratio of specific heats, adiabatic index
one_line_definition: γ = C_p/C_v; γ_dry = 1.4029 at STP; changes with humidity and CO2 concentration; controls speed of sound via c = √(γP/ρ)
Maximum partial pressure of water vapour at a given temperature; used to convert relative humidity h_r to mole fraction x_w; formula corrected in Bass 1995
Temperature change at constant volume; causes pressure to rise and relative humidity to change non-linearly; leads to non-monotone attenuation vs T at 200 kHz
Temperature change at constant pressure; volume expands, RH drops; attenuation increases linearly with T at all ACU frequencies
Temperature at which air becomes saturated and water condenses; causes anomalous ultrasonic attenuation and measurement error
Additional sound attenuation beyond geometric spreading and molecular absorption; caused by turbulent refractive index fluctuations; A_e = 10log(1 + D_t²/D_f²)
Expansion of acoustic beam diameter due to random phase fluctuations from refractive index turbulence; D_t² = 2.51∫(L-s)^(5/3) C_n²(s) ds
Coefficient of proportionality in the power-law structure function for refractive index fluctuations; characterises turbulence strength in atmospheric acoustics
Turbulence near the transmitter (s ≈ 0) contributes most to beam broadening and excess attenuation due to (L-s)^(5/3) factor
Tu = σ_v/V_mean × 100%; ratio of velocity fluctuation standard deviation to mean velocity; key parameter for draught sensation model
PD = f(T_a, V_mean, Tu); HVAC comfort model showing that turbulence intensity significantly increases draught discomfort at same mean velocity
Relative humidity inferred from measured sound speed (TOF), known temperature, and atmospheric pressure; RHU error ≤ 16.4% at 40 kHz
Most common air-coupled frequency for ranging and sensing; Nippon Ceramic PT40/PR40 and similar transducers; balances range (low α) with spatial resolution
Ranging using frequency-hopping spread-spectrum coded transmission to achieve sub-mm TOF accuracy and multipath rejection in air
Ranging technique using continuous frequency-swept signal; range derived from beat frequency between transmitted and received signal
Specific drive waveform for ultrasonic transducers that improves TOF accuracy by shaping envelope and suppressing ringing
ACU technique exploiting transducer resonance for high sensitivity; high-Q materials give narrowband high-amplitude response; Álvarez-Arenas 2019
Acoustic ranging method using commercial-off-the-shelf audio hardware (smartphones) with OFDM-like spread-spectrum signals; sub-mm accuracy
Correction of TOF-based range measurements for temperature-dependent sound speed variation; often uses separate temperature sensor or acoustic thermometer
High-frequency (≥100 MHz) focused ultrasound imaging of materials for near-surface defect and microstructure characterisation
Technique to generate a phase-conjugate replica of an acoustic wave; enables focusing back through scattering media; demonstrated in air at MHz frequencies

aliases_seen: remote surface texture sensing, non-contact roughness feel
one_line_definition: Remote detection and reproduction of surface roughness perception using airborne ultrasound pressure field distribution at 40 kHz
Spatial distribution of airborne ultrasound pressure around a rough surface used to infer roughness for remote haptic feedback

aliases_seen: non-contact roughness measurement, ultrasonic surface texture
one_line_definition: Measurement of surface topography parameters (Ra, Rq, Sa, Sq) using reflected or scattered ultrasonic signals without physical contact
Ratio of actual to expected ACU echo amplitude; drops as grinding wheel surface clogs or wears; used to trigger dressing operations
Method to characterise roughness at buried coating-substrate interface from frequency-dependent reflection coefficient; Ma 2017
Non-contact measurement of paper rms roughness using 0.5–4 MHz airborne ultrasound; compared with Bendtsen and Parker-Printsurf air-leak methods
Contact air-leak methods for paper roughness; measure macro-scale roughness under clamping pressure; weight roughness spectrum differently from ultrasonic method
Transducer with acoustic lens or curved element concentrating beam to a small spot; reduces sensitivity to sample tilt and waviness; critical for paper measurements
Distance from transducer face to specimen surface in ACU; limited by α·d < SNR budget; typically 10–100 mm at 40–500 kHz
Surface is acoustically smooth when Rq << λ/(8cosθ); for ACU at 1.8 MHz in air, λ ≈ 200 μm, so criterion is Rq << 25 μm

aliases_seen: pressure field distribution, transducer radiation pattern
one_line_definition: Spatial distribution of acoustic pressure in the near and far field of a transducer; measured to characterise transducer performance and beam width
Reconstruction of 2D cross-section of object from fan-beam projections at multiple angles using airborne ultrasound
Computation of 3D pressure field from measurements in a single plane using angular spectrum / Fourier propagation methods

aliases_seen: acoustic power transfer, wireless ultrasonic energy
one_line_definition: Transmission of electrical power wirelessly through air using focused high-intensity ultrasound; demonstrated at small distances

aliases_seen: non-contact food inspection, ultrasonic food measurement
one_line_definition: Application of contact and non-contact ultrasonics for quality control, thickness, and property measurement in food processing
Use of air-coupled ultrasound to measure lateral (non-axial) shift of a surface; Matsuya 2015 with sub-mm accuracy
Detection of small displacements (μm-mm) of objects behind opaque barriers using airborne ultrasonic interference patterns
Use of air-coupled TOF ultrasound to verify contents or wall thickness of sealed shipping containers without contact
Multi-element ultrasonic array operated with phase delays for beam steering or focusing in air; used for ACU imaging and roughness mapping
Inspection of materials for defects or property measurement without causing damage; ACU enables NDE without couplant

aliases_seen: guided plate waves, air-excited Lamb modes
one_line_definition: Elastic plate waves excited by oblique air-coupled ultrasound at the appropriate phase-matching angle; used for plate inspection
Use of multiple ultrasonic frequencies to resolve range ambiguity and improve accuracy; higher frequencies give finer resolution, lower frequencies give longer range
TOF measurement using amplitude-modulated multi-frequency signal to create long synthetic wavelength while maintaining fine range resolution
Measurement of surface properties from the amplitude of normal-incidence reflected pulse; sensitive to roughness and impedance
Error in ACU roughness or reflectometry measurement caused by angular misalignment or lateral offset of sample; quantified in Kaczmarek 2015
Measurement of height of discrete surface steps or grooves using ultrasonic angular echo distribution; Kudo 2006/2007
Short-range air-coupled sensor for detecting presence or proximity of objects; Magori & Walker 1987 type using pulse-echo TOF
Use of multiple ultrasonic TOF measurements to determine 3D position of robot end-effector or target; Marioli 1988
Sensor that infers relative humidity from measured sound speed (TOF) and temperature; van Schaik 2010, Kon 2010

aliases_seen: indoor temperature turbulence, HVAC thermal plumes
one_line_definition: Temporal and spatial variations of air temperature in occupied rooms; relevant for ACU TOF accuracy and draught sensation modelling

aliases_seen: high-angle surface mapping, TOF surface profile
one_line_definition: Reconstruction of surface profile (including steep angles up to ~70°) from multiple TOF measurements at various beam angles
Approximate formula for total scattered power from randomly rough surfaces summing contributions from independent facets; Neubauer 1963
Nonlinear propagation model accounting for diffraction, absorption, and nonlinear distortion in focused ultrasonic beams; pulse-echo amplitude errors at high drive levels

aliases_seen: surface profile reconstruction, B-scan surface mapping
one_line_definition: Extraction of 3D or 2D surface topography from time-of-flight map of reflected ultrasonic pulses
3D rms roughness parameter derived from 2D spectral matching of broadband ultrasonic reflection; Gunarathne 2000
Measurement of surface roughness during active machining (grinding, turning) without stopping the process; Shin 1995 CNC demo
Application of 1-3 piezocomposite transducers to air-coupled surface metrology; 1–5 MHz range; Gomez-Ullate 2004
ACU transducer with cylindrical lens producing a line focus rather than a point focus; useful for elongated inspection regions
Array transducer with sectorized elements enabling both air-coupled and water-immersion operation; designed for industrial/medical endoscopy applications
Focusing of air-coupled ultrasound beam using passive acoustic lens without active array elements; Gomez 2016
Combined ultrasonic proximity and contact force sensor on robotic fingertip enabling non-contact material recognition before grasping
